evals.name: greenfield-node-http-service
evals.prompt: I have a Node.js HTTP service at the path provided in the working directory. There's no docs/ folder and no README.md yet. Please generate structured documentation under docs/ (English) and a polished root README.md. The README template you use should be temporary — do not place it inside docs/. The project root for this task is the working directory you are operating in.
evals.expected_output: A complete docs/ tree (index, overview, getting-started, deployment, architecture/, domains/, reference/) and a root README.md grounded in the project's real code (Express + Knex + Postgres). No README template artifact left inside docs/.

evals.name: python-cli-readme-overwrite-declined
evals.prompt: Please document this Python CLI project. It has an existing README.md that I want to KEEP — if the workflow asks whether to overwrite the README, say NO and stop without writing one. Generate docs/ in English. The project root is the working directory.
evals.expected_output: A docs/ tree adapted to a Python CLI (mentions `myanalyzer` CLI commands, Click, pandas, pyproject.toml). The original README.md must remain UNCHANGED (its content includes 'DO NOT OVERWRITE — this is my hand-written README'). No new README written.

evals.name: existing-docs-keep-readme-only
evals.prompt: This project already has a docs/ folder with curated content I want to keep. When the workflow asks about docs/, choose 'keep as-is'. It does not have a root README.md — please generate one, adapting the template to this stack (Express + RabbitMQ workers + Nodemailer). Project root is the working directory.
evals.expected_output: The existing docs/ directory remains UNCHANGED (the original files still contain their original text, e.g. 'These docs already exist and are GOOD'). A new README.md exists at the project root, mentions the actual stack (Express, AMQP/RabbitMQ via amqplib, Nodemailer, workers), and links to docs/README.md.
